rationale: All 5 acceptance criteria met (acceptance lens). Edge-case lens found zero issues: pattern matches 30+ existing buttons using the same data-tooltip-id convention, react-tooltip v5.29.1 supports data-tooltip-place="right", z-index and positioning are correct, keydown handler untouched. Blind lens raised general concerns (tooltip provider existence, Cmd+B not shown, content duplication with aria-label) that are non-issues once repo context is applied: the global react-tooltip provider already exists and is used identically by 30+ other buttons, the existing Ctrl+B handler already supports metaKey/Cmd (out of scope for this ticket, which only asks for the 'Ctrl + B' label), and the tooltip is additive/complementary to aria-label rather than a duplicate or conflicting announcement. 1 dismissed (minor UX observation: tooltip text doesn't vary with isOpen state, acceptable per spec).

business_review.criterion: Tooltip appears showing "Toggle Sidebar (Ctrl + B)" on hover-hold
business_review.notes: data-tooltip-content added verbatim; global config has openEvents: { mouseover: true }

business_review.criterion: Tooltip styled consistently with other tooltips in the app
business_review.notes: targets the single global react-tooltip instance, same as 30+ other buttons

business_review.criterion: Tooltip disappears when cursor moves away
Tooltip content accessible/readable, no aria-label conflict
business_review.notes: aria-label unchanged and dynamic; tooltip text is additive, not duplicative

business_review.criterion: No regression to existing sidebar toggle click/keyboard behavior
business_review.notes: pure attribute addition; Ctrl+B handler and onClick untouched
